title: Cyber Crime Lawyer in Bangladesh: How to Get Legal Help
metaTitle: Cyber Crime Lawyer in Bangladesh – Digital Security Act Defence 2025
metaDescription: Charged under the Digital Security Act or Cyber Security Act in Bangladesh? Learn how a cyber crime lawyer can help — DSA cases, online defamation, hacking, and financial fraud.
heroIntro: Bangladesh has enacted strict cyber crime legislation — the Digital Security Act 2018 (DSA) and Cyber Security Act 2023 (CSA). Cases under these laws carry severe penalties including years of imprisonment. If you face a cyber crime charge or are a victim, hiring an experienced cyber crime lawyer is critical.
toc: Penalties Under the Digital Security Act
What a Cyber Crime Lawyer Does
Steps to Take If Accused of a Cyber Crime
How Victims Can File Cyber Crime Cases

sections.content:
Bangladesh cyber crime prosecution primarily relies on:

  • Cyber Security Act 2023 (CSA): Replaced parts of the DSA 2018. Covers hacking, identity theft, unauthorised access, online fraud, and provocative digital content.
  • Digital Security Act 2018 (DSA): Still active for offences committed before the CSA. Widely criticised for wide-ranging provisions criminalising digital content.
  • Information and Communication Technology (ICT) Act 2006 (as amended): Earlier law, particularly Section 57, under which many older cases were filed.
  • Penal Code 1860: Financial fraud, cheating, and forgery offences committed digitally are often charged under the Penal Code as well.

Contact a criminal lawyer in Dhaka immediately if a case is filed against you under any of these laws.

sections.h2: Penalties Under the Digital Security Act
sections.content:
DSA/CSA penalties are severe:

  • Hacking or unauthorised access: Up to 7–14 years imprisonment
  • Propagating offensive/false information: 3–10 years + fine
  • Identity theft: Up to 5 years
  • Online sexual harassment/defamation: Up to 5–7 years
  • Critical information infrastructure attack: Up to 14 years or life

Many offences under the DSA are non-bailable, meaning the accused can be held in custody pending trial. Immediate bail application through the High Court is often necessary.

sections.h2: What a Cyber Crime Lawyer Does
sections.content:
An experienced cyber crime lawyer in Bangladesh will:

  • Analyse the FIR/complaint and assess defences available under DSA/CSA
  • Apply for bail immediately — DSA cases are notorious for lengthy pre-trial detention
  • Challenge the technical evidence (metadata, IP addresses, digital forensics reports)
  • File writ petitions to quash cases that are politically motivated or factually baseless
  • Negotiate with the complainant for settlement in compoundable cases
  • Defend at trial in the Cyber Tribunal

sections.h2: Steps to Take If Accused of a Cyber Crime
sections.content:
1. Do not make any statement to police or cyber tribunal investigators without legal advice.
  2. Preserve all evidence including screenshots, messages, posts — both the allegedly offending content and any evidence of your innocence or intent.
  3. Hire a cyber crime lawyer immediately — even before arrest, if you hear a case is being filed.
  4. Apply for anticipatory bail if arrest is imminent.
  5. Document your alibi and gather witnesses who can attest to your whereabouts or account credentials security.

Advocate Md. Shah Alam in Uttara handles DSA/CSA defence cases in Dhaka.

sections.h2: How Victims Can File Cyber Crime Cases
sections.content:
If you are a victim of online fraud, hacking, defamation, or harassment in Bangladesh:

  • File an FIR at your local police station or the Cyber Crime Investigation Department (CCID) of Bangladesh Police (Agargaon, Dhaka).
  • CCID has a dedicated online complaint portal at cybercrime.gov.bd.
  • Preserve all evidence: screenshots, URLs, chat logs, transaction records with timestamps.
  • A lawyer can help you draft a stronger complaint that accurately identifies the legal provisions violated and ensures evidence is collected in an admissible form.

faqs.question: Is a cyber crime case bailable in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Most DSA/CSA offences are non-bailable, meaning police cannot grant bail — it must be sought from a court (Sessions Court or High Court). Immediate legal representation is essential to apply for bail.

faqs.question: What court hears cyber crime cases in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Cyber crime cases are heard in designated Cyber Tribunals established under the ICT Act and DSA. There are Cyber Tribunals in Dhaka and other divisional cities.

faqs.question: Can a defamatory Facebook post result in a DSA case in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. Section 29 of the DSA (defamation) has been frequently used to file cases over social media posts. If you are accused under this provision, bail and quashing proceedings may be available.

faqs.question: Can someone file a DSA case against me anonymously?
faqs.answer: Yes. Complaints can be filed at police stations or online. However, in court proceedings, the complainant must be identified and can be cross-examined.
